C001.claim: # Dispersion ## The core idea Dispersion is when waves of different wavelengths (like colors of light) travel at different speeds through a medium, causing them to separate.
C001.query: Dispersion # Dispersion ## The core idea Dispersion is when waves of different wavelengths (like colors of physics lecture notes textbook explanation
C001.result_snippet: Dispersion in Physics: Splitting Light by Wavelength. In optics, dispersion is the spreading of white light into its full spectrum of wavelengths.This happens because light of different wavelengths (colors) bends by different amounts when it enters a new medium like glass or water. Causes of Dispersion. Because each colors' light has distinct wavelengths, white light disperses into seven colors. Red light has the longest wavelength in this range of seven colors, while violet light has the shortest. In a vacuum, all colors of light travel at the same speed. Dispersion and diffraction are two ways light waves distribute themselves in patterns.When white light hits the refracting surface, the different colors of light separate because of their differing wavelengths. Nature of Dispersion. Dispersion occurs when light separates into its individual colors as it passes through a medium, such as a prism. When white light enters the prism, it bends at different angles depending on each color’s wavelength. Dispersion: When Different Wavelengths Bend Differently. The Role of the Medium (Pane).2. Why do different colors (wavelengths) of light bend at different angles?4. How does the angle of incidence affect dispersion?

C002.claim: This happens because the **refractive index** {n} of a material varies with wavelength—shorter wavelengths (blue light) slow more than longer ones (red light), making them bend at different angles.

C003.claim: Imagine a prism splitting sunlight into a rainbow: this is dispersion in action.
C003.query: Dispersion Imagine a prism splitting sunlight into a rainbow: this is dispersion in action. physics lecture notes textbook explanation
C003.result_snippet: Rainbows are formed by dispersion of light, in which the refraction angle depends on the light's frequency. Refraction is also responsible for rainbows and for the splitting of white light into a rainbow-spectrum as it passes through a glass prism. Dispersion of light is the phenomenon in which white light splits into its constituent colors when it passes through a prism. This happens because each color of light has a different wavelength and bends by a different amount upon refraction. If you've ever marvelled at a rainbow, you have witnessed dispersion in action. Dispersion is where the speed at which a wave moves depends on its frequency (and so wavelength). Watch: The Physics of Light Dispersion in Action.Who first proved that prisms split light? When light passes through a prism, it undergoes refraction and dispersion, causing white light to separate into its constituent colors—the visible spectrum. What is Dispersion. Dispersion occurs when light enters a material and spreads out into its spectrum of wavelengths.The water acts as a prism, splitting the light into its constituent colours.
